question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted timestamp Bound with operator GT set to Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 to trigger integer overflow in getAdjustedLowerRangeValue(), causing minTimestamp to wrap negative and bypass the minTimestamp > maxTimestamp guard, returning supply data from an unintended historical range and corrupting the integrity of reported circulating supply?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Bound where adjustUpperBound() returns Long.MAX_VALUE and getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() returns 0, causing accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory() to scan the entire historical dataset, exhausting database resources and denying supply data to legitimate transaction validators?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp Bound with LT operator set to 1 so that adjustUpperBound() returns 0 and getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() returns 0, making minTimestamp == maxTimestamp == 0 and causing getSupplyHistory() to return a zero consensusTimestamp, triggering EntityNotFoundException and disrupting supply reporting for submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a future timestamp far beyond the current consensus time so that getFirstDayOfMonth(maxTimestamp, -1) computes an optimalLowerBound that is still in the future, causing accountBalanceRepository.getSupplyHistory() to return no rows and networkSupply.consensusTimestamp() == 0, throwing EntityNotFoundException and making supply data unavailable for transaction integrity checks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Bound with both lower and upper set such that Math.max(minTimestamp, optimalLowerBound) silently discards the user-supplied lower bound and returns supply data from a different time window than requested, causing clients relying on this data for transaction verification to receive stale or incorrect circulating supply figures?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a timestamp Bound using the EQ operator (which Bound.of() rewrites to LTE) with a value of 0, causing adjustUpperBound() to return 0 and getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() to return 0, so that the supply query window collapses to a single nanosecond at epoch zero and returns no valid supply data, breaking transaction-level supply integrity checks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSupply(Bound timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly submit high-cardinality timestamp range queries to getSupply() with maxTimestamp near Long.MAX_VALUE, causing getFirstDayOfMonth() to invoke Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, timestamp) with an out-of-range nanosecond value, triggering a DateTimeException that crashes the service thread and denies supply data to all concurrent transaction processors?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFirstDayOfMonth(long timestamp, int monthOffset)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a maxTimestamp corresponding to January of year 1 so that plusMonths(-1) underflows to December of year 0 (year BCE), causing getFirstDayOfMonth() to throw a DateTimeException or return a negative epoch value that corrupts the minTimestamp floor used in getSupplyHistory(), leading to incorrect supply data being returned for submitted transaction validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getFirstDayOfMonth(long timestamp, int monthOffset)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a timestamp that when converted via Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, timestamp) and atZone(ZoneOffset.UTC) produces a date in a DST-ambiguous zone, causing getFirstDayOfMonth() to compute an incorrect epoch boundary that shifts the supply query window and returns supply figures inconsistent with the actual on-chain state at the time of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with a nodeId EQ parameter whose value falls outside the lowerBound-upperBound range, causing the nodeIdArray filter to return an empty array and getNetworkNodes() to return List.of() silently, making a valid node appear absent and causing transaction routing logic that depends on this API to skip that node entirely?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with conflicting nodeId range parameters (e.g., GT:5 and LT:3) so that lowerBound > upperBound, triggering the IllegalArgumentException path and causing a denial of service for any client that relies on node discovery to route submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with a fileId parameter pointing to an arbitrary entity ID (not the canonical addressBookFile102), causing getAddressBookFileId() to use the attacker-supplied fileId and networkNodeRepository.findNetworkNodes() to query a different address book, returning a tampered or stale node list that misleads transaction submission clients about the current network topology?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getAddressBookFileId(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a fileId with an arbitrary entity ID value in the NetworkNodeRequest, bypassing the default systemEntity.addressBookFile102() selection in getAddressBookFileId(), and causing networkNodeRepository.findNetworkNodes() to return nodes from an outdated or attacker-influenced address book, corrupting the node set used for transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with limit set to Integer.MAX_VALUE, and since getEffectiveLimit() caps at MAX_LIMIT (25) but the underlying networkNodeRepository.findNetworkNodes() receives this capped value, can the attacker still craft repeated concurrent requests to exhaust database connection pool resources and deny node discovery to legitimate transaction submitters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with multiple EQ nodeId parameters that all pass the Range.closed(lowerBound, upperBound) filter, causing nodeIdArray to contain many entries and networkNodeRepository.findNetworkNodes() to execute a large IN-clause query, exhausting database query plan resources and degrading node discovery for transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with nodeId GTE:0 and nodeId LTE:Long.MAX_VALUE, causing lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE with an empty nodeIds set, so that networkNodeRepository.findNetworkNodes() is called with EMPTY_NODE_ID_ARRAY and the full node range, potentially returning all nodes in the address book and leaking the complete network topology used for transaction routing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getNetworkNodes(NetworkNodeRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a NetworkNodeRequest with orderDirection set to a crafted string value that bypasses Direction enum validation and is passed raw to networkNodeRepository.findNetworkNodes() as an ORDER BY clause fragment, enabling SQL injection that tampers with the node result set returned to transaction routing clients?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with registeredNodeIds containing an EQ operator combined with additional range operators, bypassing the resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() check that only throws when registeredNodeIdRanges.size() > 1 with EQ, and causing the repository to return an unintended set of registered nodes that affects transaction node selection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with two range parameters both using GTE operator, causing resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() to apply Math.max() twice on lowerBound without detecting the duplicate, silently accepting an ambiguous lower bound and returning a node set that does not match the intended query semantics for transaction node validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with a GTE:Long.MAX_VALUE parameter, causing resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() to set lowerBound = Long.MAX_VALUE and upperBound = Long.MAX_VALUE (default), so that findByRegisteredNodeIdBetweenAndDeletedIsFalseAndTypeIs() returns an empty result, making all registered nodes appear absent and disrupting transaction node selection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds(List<NumberRangeParameter> registeredNodeIdRanges)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with LTE:0 as the only range parameter, causing resolveRegisteredNodeIdBounds() to set upperBound = 0 and lowerBound = 0 (default), so that only node ID 0 is queried, hiding all other registered nodes from the response and misleading transaction clients about available nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with limit set to MAX_LIMIT and order set to DESC, combined with a registeredNodeId range covering all IDs, causing the repository to return the highest-ID registered nodes first and leaking information about recently registered nodes that may not yet be publicly announced, affecting transaction submission trust decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getRegisteredNodes(RegisteredNodesRequest request)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RegisteredNodesRequest with type set to a valid RegisteredNodeType enum value to filter nodes by type, and by iterating through all enum values in parallel requests, enumerate the complete set of registered nodes by type, leaking network topology information that could be used to target specific node types for transaction tampering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NetworkService.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getLatestNetworkStake()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated calls to getLatestNetworkStake() at high frequency, causing networkStakeRepository.findLatest() to execute repeated full-table scans or index lookups on the network_stake table, exhausting database I/O and denying staking data to transaction validators that rely on current stake weights for consensus participation decisions?
